name: agentic-rules
description: Structured behavioral rules for AI agents: persistent memory, RAG/context discipline, critical-thinking heuristics, and interaction unit-testing. Delivered as auto-invocable skills, with an optional always-on injector and a Knowledge Graph MCP server.

language.title: Rule language
language.description: Language for injected rule text (always-on mode). Accepted: en, ja, id.

memory_path.title: Memory root path
memory_path.description: Root directory for the persistent memory store. Creates common/, private/, and <project>/ subfolders. Leave blank to let the memory skill ask on first use.

enable_memory.title: Enable memory rules
enable_memory.description: Persistent cross-session memory (store/recall facts, decisions, corrections).

enable_rag.title: Enable RAG / context rules
enable_rag.description: Hierarchical reading, relevance scoring, and source-grounded responses.

enable_critical_thinking.title: Enable critical-thinking rules
enable_critical_thinking.description: Challenge vague requirements, ground-check claims, calibrate uncertainty, prevent hallucination.

enable_agent_unit_test.title: Enable agent interaction unit-test rules
enable_agent_unit_test.description: Compliance/ground-check auditing of agent conversations. Off by default; invoke explicitly.

always_on_injection.title: Always-on rule injection
always_on_injection.description: When on (default), a SessionStart hook injects an imperative activation directive plus the enabled rule text into every session (heavier token cost, closest to a CLAUDE.md, and the only reliable way to activate the rules — skill auto-invocation loses to the model's native memory). Turn off to load rules on demand as skills instead.

kg_mcp_url.title: Knowledge Graph MCP endpoint
kg_mcp_url.description: HTTP URL of your Knowledge Graph MCP server. Clear this to disable the KG server; the memory and RAG skills degrade gracefully without it.
